title: Public and private goods
description: Practice what you've learned about public and private goods in this exercise. Topics include the definitions of excludability and rivalry, and how to identify public goods, private goods, artificially scarce goods, and common resources based on excludability and rivalry.
topic_path: Market failure and the role of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**What kinds of goods are excludable?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Private goods and artificially scarce goods

assessment_items.question_content:
**When an economist says that a good is rival in consumption, what does that mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: only one agent can consume one unit of the good
a person can be kept from consuming a good if they have not paid for it.
consumption of a good confers benefits to someone else not consuming the good
consumption of a good incurs costs to someone not consuming or producing it
the right to consume a good or transfer a good

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes something nonrival in consumption?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: watching a streaming video service with unlimited bandwidth
a seat on an airplane going to a tropical beach
a dance party in a small venue that requires a ticket to be let in
five friends splitting gas money to drive to school

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the free rider problem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When goods are nonexcludable, people have no incentive to pay for their production
When goods are non-rival, they tend to be overconsumed
When production of a good creates costs that are not considered by producers
When consumption of a good creates benefits that are not considered by consumers
When the political or social environment is changed to extract more profit without contributing any additional benefit

assessment_items.question_content:
The Cliffs of Hamsterville are a popular tourist attraction, however as more people visit them the paths get worn down and it gets more difficult to see them. The Cliffs are on land owned by the government, and there are currently no restrictions on who can visit them.

**What kind of good are the Cliffs of Hamsterville, and what issue does it face?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Common resource; the tragedy of the commons
Public good; the free rider problem
Private goods; markets cannot provide this good efficiently
Positive externalities; inefficiently low consumption of the good

assessment_items.question_content:
Basic research is non-excludable and non-rival. However, the government of Hamsterville has noticed that innovators are preferring to go to the beach instead of the lab because it takes time to develop new processes and products, but the innovators do not get any benefit out of it. 

As a result, the government has created a patent system that gives inventors the right to be the sole producer of anything invented for $15$ years.

**When the government created the patent system, what kind of good did basic research effectively become?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The government of Jacksonia produces and sells cream cheese. Nobody is allowed to consume cream cheese without paying for it, and only one person can consume a given block of cream cheese.

**What kind of good is cream cheese?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Because no private provider was willing to do so, the city of Montrose has decided to provide emergency room services free of charge. However, a doctor can only see one patient at a time.

**What kind of good is the emergency room service in Montrose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

HamsterCraft is a multiplayer online game. When too many people try to play at the same time the server crashes. There is no charge to play.

**What kind of good is this game?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What must be true about private goods?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are only produced by monopolies
They do not exist in centrally planned economies
They suffer from the tragedy of the commons

assessment_items.question_content:
**The problem called the “tragedy of the commons” is associated with what kind of good?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following government policies would address the problem of overuse of a common resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: create a licensing system to limit access
allow free access to anyone who wants to use it
remove property rights to the resource
